Describe what is happening to the raw chicken on the cutting board?
canonical_answer: Raw chicken drumsticks and wings with pink skin rest on a light wooden cutting board. A yellowish viscous liquid containing red pepper flakes is poured over the clustered pieces. The marinade coats the skin, creating a glossy texture with visible dark specks. Liquid pools around the base of the chicken arrangement.

question: Describe the sequence of events involving the raw chicken and marinade application.
canonical_answer: At 10s, raw chicken pieces with pink skin appear on a beige surface; at 11s, a stream of yellowish viscous liquid is drizzled over the pile; at 15s, raw drumsticks are arranged in rows on a wooden board; at 16s, yellowish liquid with red pepper flakes is poured over the arranged meat.

question: Describe the visual details observed in the cooked chicken segments.
canonical_answer: At 2s, multiple cooked chicken drumsticks with golden-brown skin appear piled together; at 3s, dark seasoning speckles are visible on the crispy surface; at 7s, the chicken pieces show a glossy sheen on the skin; at 9s, charred spots are visible on the glistening skin of the thighs and drumsticks.
